danger_signals.visual_form: Demonic beasts (ogres, armor-clad knights), possessed soldiers rendered as hostile entities
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player; combat engagement required

danger_signals.visual_form: Gothic and medieval architecture in decay; science fiction military base deterioration
danger_signals.meaning: Unsafe spaces; contamination or invasion aftermath

danger_signals.visual_form: Mix of futuristic military and demonic/fantasy elements in same space
danger_signals.meaning: World has been compromised; normal order is disrupted by hostile force

safety_signals.visual_form: Level exit points (marked spatially as progression targets)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression; completion of current area

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapons and power-ups placed throughout levels

safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; choice and control restored to player

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Futuristic military base architecture and design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Possessed soldiers rendered as corrupted human forms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Science fiction military facilities; technological infrastructure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ogres, demonic beasts, armor-clad knights, monsters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Gothic and medieval environments; slipgate technology (teleportation portals)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Military bases represent human control; organized futuristic architecture
Fusion of military bases with medieval gothic environments; architectural contamination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile monsters throughout levels; soldiers possessed and corrupted; player must fight through occupied spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Teleportation portals connecting human bases to demonic realms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must traverse between science fiction and fantasy environments; traversal through slipgates indicates world connectivity under invasion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Recovery of magic runes as artifacts; return to hub level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player resets inventory at each episode start; narrative progression through rune collection

environmental_storytelling.example: Juxtaposition of futuristic military base architecture with medieval gothic imagery; tells story of invasion via spatial mixing
Possessed soldiers in military bases; demonic beasts in gothic areas; suggests origin and spread of invasion
Hidden 'Nightmare' difficulty indicates danger escalation; narrative framing as insurmountable threat
Four episodes each culminate in rune recovery; final puzzle in END level; tells story of dimensional sealing/containment
Anomalous physics in secret areas; suggests reality distortion from invasion

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attack player on sight; rendered as corrupted human forms
Patrol and attack; native to gothic environments; indicate alien threat
Explores, collects items, recovers runes, traverses slipgates

notes: Quake communicates world-state primarily through environmental contrast (military vs. gothic), enemy type placement, and spatial design. The game establishes a two-faction system: human/military (compromised) vs. demonic invaders (ascendant). Danger is signaled through enemy presence and architectural decay. Safety is implied through exit points and hub-level returns. The slipgate mechanic visually represents the invasion mechanism itself. Rune collection provides narrative structure for world-state progression (seven runes = sealing the invasion). The game heavily uses Lovecraftian and H.P. Lovecraft-inspired aesthetics to communicate existential threat.
